source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper. M0041 rewrote Section 2 to introduce the no-noise SVAR, recovered shocks, sign restrictions, and then the noisy model in SVAR-reader language.

For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two. Section 4 places this assumption after the residual-noise-to-signal covariance screen and, after M55, explains why higher cumulants are blind to Gaussian noise at B0.

Scale must be fixed once, not twice. M0034 flags that the manuscript cannot simultaneously use the diagonal-normalized chart diag(B)=1 and also impose unit shock variances when deriving second-order restrictions. M0038 Section 4 sketches the active route: profile shock variances and residual-noise variances in a diagonal-normalized chart, while keeping pure higher cumulants as the validity backbone. M54 audited the requested transformed-noise moments, retained the diag(B)=1 chart for the first paper, and treated the source-native DW unit-variance scaling as internal recovered-shock standardization rather than a manuscript-wide rewrite.

Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0. M0041 rewrote this as a noisy sign pseudo-set after first presenting the no-noise recovered-shock orthogonality condition.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: No-noise sign-restricted set written as a J-test-style orthogonality condition over recovered shocks: candidates satisfy R(B) >= 0 and E[e_1(B)e_2(B)] = 0 after sign, label, and scale normalizations. Added to make the uncorrelated-shock restriction explicit for SVAR readers.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M0041 revision: the standard covariance-factor sign set reports factors of B0 B0' + V, so B0 itself is included only when V = 0; up to positive column rescaling, the true sign shape survives only in structural-coordinate rescaling cases where B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'} is diagonal.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise. M0038 inserted the equation with label eq:column-rescaling-obstruction in Section 2.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M49 source-audited the bivariate Drautzburg-Wright higher-moment menus. GMM1 uses standardized raw products 112, 122, 1112, 1122, and 1222; GMM2 drops only the symmetric 1122 kurtosis condition. M0050 replaced the draft's temporary h_i(B) notation with the already defined recovered-shock notation e_t(B), while noting the source-standardized centering and unit-variance scaling needed outside the source-native rotation chart. In the manuscript's common B-plane chart, M52 implements the source-correct GMM1 higher-moment menu and treats the no-noise covariance restriction as a separate B-plane screen, not a DW higher-moment entry.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; raw/drautzburg-2023-refining-set-identification/Refining_set-identification_in_VARs_through_independence.md; manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md

objects.description: M25 working result, rewritten in M0041: under residual noise, a standard no-noise DW stack imposes recovered-shock covariance and higher-order independence moments on e(B)=B^{-1}u. A rich population stack has zeros only in structural-coordinate rescaling cases, up to finite-moment aliases; otherwise fixed-critical-value inversion empties asymptotically and finite samples can concentrate near least-rejected noisy pseudo-targets.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; M49 source-audited the relevant DW GMM1/GMM2 moment menus; M52 rebuilt the evidence path with GMM1 plus a separate covariance screen; M47 still needs to audit the rich-stack generic emptying and finite-stack alias claims.

objects.description: Sample J-test inversion for the standard no-noise DW moment stack. M49 source-audited that bivariate DW GMM1 uses 112, 122, 1112, 1122, and 1222, while GMM2 drops only 1122. M0050 now displays this menu with e_t(B) recovered-shock notation rather than h_i(B). The draft separates the source menu from any B-plane covariance screen. M52 implements GMM1 in the figure and Monte Carlo code, with covariance applied as a separate B-plane screen.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; M49 source and noisy-moment audit complete; M52 evidence rebuild complete; M47 proof audit remains before theorem-level wording.

objects.description: Variance-ratio robust DW set that searches over a normalized impact space, imposes sign restrictions and the covariance-decomposition screen 0 <= nu_i <= rho s_i, and uses mixed higher-order moment conditions for B^{-1}u rather than no-noise recovered-shock covariance. M0050 rewrote Section 4 so the reader-facing display uses explicit moment-condition notation rather than foregrounding cumulant notation. M56 adds that the sample fourth-cumulant entries are generated smooth moments, and M52 implements the full central-moment delta-weighting route for the active evidence.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for the pure local normalized Gaussian-noise higher-cumulant route. M0030/M37 added and audited a six-moment diagonal-anchor variant, but M0034 supersedes that variant: under diag(B)=1 the off-diagonal covariance is Sigma_u,12=b21*sigma_1^2+b12*sigma_2^2 unless unit shock variances are imposed as an extra scale normalization. M0036 supplies the variance-ratio proposal, M40 conditionally passed its covariance-screen algebra and interpretation, and M52 rebuilt lightweight figure, validation, and Monte Carlo evidence around it. M56 conditionally passes the generated-moment route, and M52 implements full central-moment delta influence rows with mean-centering nuisance terms.

objects.description: Displayed moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack after M0050 and M55: G_H(B) lists third mixed central moment conditions and fourth mixed higher-order conditions of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with transformed second moments entering directly through covariance-product subtractions. M49 rederived the requested noisy product moments and confirmed the contrast: third-order robust conditions coincide with centered raw third products under Gaussian residual noise, while fourth-order robust conditions differ from DW raw fourth product moments by covariance-product subtractions. M55 now distinguishes transformed-noise covariance Omega(B)=Var(B^{-1}eta_t) from full transformed-residual covariance S(B)=Var(B^{-1}u_t), because the fourth-order subtractions use S(B) computed from z(B), not the unobserved noise covariance alone. M56 resolves the generated-moment issue at the derivation level: sample fourth-cumulant entries are smooth functions of primitive sample moments, with primitive/delta or augmented nuisance-covariance inference routes. The off-diagonal residual covariance anchor is no longer part of the active robust stack under diag(B)=1.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth-order covariance-product subtractions. M0034 invalidated the added M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor under the active scale normalization, so the active pure stack has five moments. M49 completed the standard-product versus robust-fourth-condition comparison and noisy moment derivations. M0050 rewrote the displayed Section 4 notation without changing this mathematical object. M54 supplied the stepwise transformed-noise derivation; M55 turned that derivation into reader-facing Section 4 prose and prevents S/Omega notation confusion. M56 shows the concentrated sample cumulants are generated smooth moments, not ordinary primitive row-level moments. M52 implements full central-moment delta influence rows with mean-centering nuisance terms for the active evidence.

objects.description: M0035 optional covariance screen: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]] and e(B)=B^{-1}u, diagonal residual noise V=diag(nu_1,nu_2) implies E[e1 e2]=(-b*nu_1-a*nu_2)/(1-ab)^2 at the candidate truth. With 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5, the sample recovered covariance must lie in the induced candidate-specific interval.
objects.verification_status: M0035 figure diagnostic generated; the absolute bound is now a historical comparison after M0036/M40 because its upper cap is scale-arbitrary. Do not use it as the active theorem-level object without a separate audit.

objects.description: M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal, now placed at the start of Section 4 after M0041: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]], sample covariance S, structural-shock variances s_i, and diagonal residual-noise variances nu_i, require S11=s1+a^2*s2+nu1, S12=b*s1+a*s2, S22=b^2*s1+s2+nu2, and 0 <= nu_i <= rho*s_i. The draft describes rho as a researcher-chosen maximum residual-noise-to-signal ratio, with rho=0.5 in the simulations.
objects.verification_status: M40 conditional pass: the covariance-screen algebra and implementation match the profiled variance-ratio model, and the 50 percent bound is defensible only as explicit signal-to-noise identifying information. M52 rebuilt lightweight figures, validation, and Monte Carlo evidence; the hard sample screen can still exclude truth in finite samples and needs careful final-claim wording.

objects.description: Working result, rewritten in M0041 and cleaned up in M0050/M55: under additive Gaussian residual noise, the mixed higher-order robust moment conditions for B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise once the fourth-order covariance-product subtractions are included. M54 derived this step by step at B=B0, and M55 now explains in the main text why the conditions hold, why the covariance-product terms use full transformed-residual covariances S_ij(B), and how those entries are computed from z_t(B)=B^{-1}u_t. M56 resolves the finite-sample inference issue at the derivation level: concentrated sample cumulants are nonlinear functions of primitive sample moments and require primitive-moment delta covariance, augmented nuisance covariance moments, or calibration for final evidence. In the bivariate normalized system, local higher-moment identification follows when each structural shock has informative third or fourth higher-order entries. If true diagonal residual-noise variances satisfy nu_i <= rho s_i, the true impact shape also satisfies the variance-ratio covariance screen.
objects.proof_status: Pure higher-cumulant local-rank audit passed conditionally in M24. M0030/M37 diagonal-anchor pass judgment is superseded by M0034 scale correction. M40 conditionally passed the M0036 variance-ratio screen as a population feasibility restriction. M52 rebuilt lightweight finite-sample evidence and Figures 1-3. M54 completed the transformed-noise moment derivation, M55 completed the main-text B0 validity and S(B)-versus-Omega(B) explanation, M56 completed the generated-moment audit, and M52 implemented central-delta generated-moment weighting. Final theorem wording still needs proof review and final replication treatment.

objects.description: Diagnostic statement after M52: standard-DW accepted mass outside the variance-ratio robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while a large robust-DW set records the information lost by dropping invalid recovered-shock covariance restrictions and profiling diagonal noise.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete for the comparison metric. M52 recomputed the metric bundle for the source-correct standard-DW GMM1 set and variance-ratio robust set after Figures 1-3 were rebuilt.

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle for the common normalized chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.
Intuitive sign-set geometry is now covered by the top row of the M0036 relative-noise residual grid, which shows sign/covariance accepted sets moving as residual noise increases.
objects.output_source: Proposal visual spine: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py --robust-mode relative and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png. A separate covariance-ellipse panel is optional, not required for the first draft.

objects.description: Residual-noise story figure after M52. The standard-DW row uses source-correct bivariate GMM1 higher moments plus a separate covariance screen in the common B-plane. The variance-ratio robust row uses generated higher-cumulant J inversion with full central-delta weighting plus a covariance-decomposition feasibility screen with 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5 Var(epsilon_i). In the high-noise fixed draw, standard DW misses true B0 while robust DW contains it.
objects.output_source: M0036 relative variant: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_noise_grid_figure.py --robust-mode relative, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid_figure.md, and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. M0035 absolute-bound comparison remains manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid.png. M0034 pure variant remains manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_pure_robust_noise_grid.png. The earlier diagonal-anchor output manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_noise_grid.png is superseded for the robust row.

objects.description: Grid story after M52. Figure 1 uses the variance-ratio robust DW proposal and source-correct standard-DW GMM1 screen, Figure 2 uses the same rows while varying structural non-Gaussianity, and Figure 3 varies sample size. The standard-DW rows no longer use the old hybrid source menu.
objects.output_source: Figure 1: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png and manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid_figure.md. Figure 2: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid.png and manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_robust_nongaussianity_grid_figure.py. Figure 3: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_sample_size_robust_grid.png and manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_sample_size_robust_grid_figure.py. M52 validation output: manuscript/simulations/m52_source_correct_evidence.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m52_source_correct_evidence.json. Source-correct standard-DW rebuild packet: manuscript/tasks/M52-standard-dw-source-correct-rebuild.md.

objects.description: Figure 3 after M52: a sample-size grid with T=500, T=1000, and T=2000, holding the Figure 1 structural non-Gaussianity calibration and Figure 2 residual-noise calibration fixed. In the fixed draw, source-correct standard-DW GMM1 misses the truth at larger sample sizes while variance-ratio robust DW remains truth-containing.

M52 chi-square-primary Monte Carlo summary for the source-correct standard-DW GMM1 screen versus the variance-ratio robust DW proposal. The hard covariance-decomposition screen is applied to accepted grid points and truth-inclusion calculations. Primary high-noise row: standard-DW truth inclusion 0.000, robust-DW truth inclusion 0.833, robust truth-feasible rate 0.958.
objects.output_source: M52 output: manuscript/simulations/m45_variance_ratio_evidence.py, manuscript/simulations/m52_source_correct_evidence.md, and manuscript/simulations/output/m52_source_correct_evidence.json. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. M56 audit: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md. Superseded historical M45 and M29 outputs remain for audit history.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW higher-cumulant derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded. M37 later audited the post-M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor as a separate six-moment estimator gate.

Direct post-M0030 audit of the reported diagonal-noise robust DW estimator. Superseded by M0034: the off-diagonal covariance anchor is not valid in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are also imposed. The pure higher-cumulant part remains the active valid route under independent Gaussian residual noise.

M40 adversarial audit of the M0036 variance-ratio covariance screen. Conditional pass: the profiled covariance-decomposition algebra and implementation match the normalized bivariate chart, and the 50 percent noise-to-shock variance bound is defensible only as explicit signal-to-noise identifying information. M52 has now rebuilt lightweight figures, validation, and Monte Carlo evidence; the hard finite-sample screen still needs careful prose and final replication treatment before final claims.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

Refreshed M28 validation gate for the M0030 revised grid pair. It is historical after M0034/M0036 because the robust row used the superseded diagonal-anchor statistic. M52 supplies the active variance-ratio evidence gate.

M52 validation and Monte Carlo evidence for the M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal and source-correct standard-DW GMM1 comparator. The run rebuilds fixed-grid diagnostics for Figures 1-3 and a chi-square-primary Monte Carlo table with 60 calibration replications, 24 evaluation replications, and a 41-by-41 grid. It applies the hard relative covariance screen in every accepted-set and truth-inclusion calculation. The robust row uses full central-moment delta influence rows for generated fourth-cumulant moments.

M49 source and derivation audit. It establishes the exact source-correct bivariate Drautzburg-Wright GMM1 menu as 112, 122, 1112, 1122, and 1222, with GMM2 dropping only 1122; classified the then-current Figure 1/M45 standard-DW code as a historical hybrid; derives the requested noisy product moments from z=epsilon+B0^{-1}eta; and leaves a full unit-variance/rotation-chart switch as a user-decision gate. M52 later implemented the GMM1 rebuild.

M56 generated-moment audit. It shows that robust fourth-cumulant sample entries such as mean(z1*z2^3)-3 mean(z2^2) mean(z1*z2) are smooth functions of primitive sample moments, not ordinary fixed row-level GMM moments. The population cumulant restrictions remain valid under independent Gaussian residual noise, but final finite-sample evidence should use primitive-moment delta-method covariance including centering terms, an equivalent augmented nuisance-covariance GMM system, or bootstrap/repeated-sample calibration. M52 implements the full central-moment delta route for the active evidence.
objects.source: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md; KnowledgeVault/vault/papers/A Generalized Method of Moments Estimator for Structural Vector Autoregressions Based on Higher Moments.md; KnowledgeVault/svar-toolkit/docs/api/gmm.md

open_audits.question: Now that M52 has repaired the source-correct standard-DW evidence path, does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?
